title: Context file frontmatter
description: YAML frontmatter required on every .context/**/*.md file.

title.description: Human-readable title matching the H1 heading.

type.description: File category (UPPER_CASE) — matches the subdirectory (plans/ → PLAN, findings/ → FINDING, analysis/ → ANALYSIS, known-issues/ → KNOWN_ISSUE). Note KNOWN_ISSUE uses an underscore while its directory is known-issues/.

status.description: Lifecycle status (UPPER_CASE). Use DRAFT until reviewed, ACTIVE for in-progress work you would pick up next, DEFERRED for a real not-done item that cannot be actioned yet because it is date-gated or externally blocked (NOT for merely low-priority work — that is expressed by value: LOW on an ACTIVE item), DONE when complete, SUPERSEDED when replaced. The 'what's next' read protocol ranks DEFERRED strictly below DRAFT/ACTIVE.

date.description: Creation date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Do not update on subsequent edits.

deferred_until.description: Optional reactivation date (YYYY-MM-DD) for a date-gated DEFERRED item — the date on or after which it should be reconsidered for reactivation to ACTIVE. Only meaningful with status: DEFERRED (validate-context-frontmatter.sh rejects it on any other status). Omit for externally-blocked items with no known ripen date. The read protocol does NOT list a DEFERRED item whose deferred_until is still in the future — it is hidden from the pick until that date passes, at which point it surfaces as reactivation-eligible. The date takes precedence even if the item is also externally blocked.

related.description: Relative paths to related .context files. Omit the field entirely if there are no related files.

effort.description: T-shirt-sized total implementation effort, matching the skill-auditor remediation-plan convention (S/M/L). Required for type: PLAN while status is DRAFT or ACTIVE (enforced by validate-context-frontmatter.sh, not this schema alone, since JSON Schema conditionals aren't evaluated by that validator). Use TBD only when sizing is genuinely blocked on an unresolved item in Open Questions. Not applicable to FINDING/ANALYSIS/INSTRUCTION/AUDIT. Set once at creation like date; re-size only if scope materially changes.

severity.description: Urgency of a KNOWN_ISSUE entry (UPPER_CASE). Required for type: KNOWN_ISSUE (enforced by validate-context-frontmatter.sh, not this schema alone). Not applicable to other types. Set once at creation; re-grade only if the issue's real-world impact turns out to differ from the initial assessment.

value.description: Benefit-of-action signal (UPPER_CASE): how much doing this item unblocks or leverages future work, graded against the rubric in .context/instructions/value-rubric.md. Applies to type: PLAN, FINDING, and KNOWN_ISSUE (the action-candidate types). Distinct from severity (risk-of-inaction) and effort (cost-of-action). The 'what's next' read protocol sorts by value descending, then effort ascending. Required for those three types while status is DRAFT or ACTIVE (enforced by validate-context-frontmatter.sh, not this schema alone, since JSON Schema conditionals aren't evaluated by that validator). Re-grade on status transitions or material scope changes, per ways-of-working.md.

themes.description: Subject axis (UPPER_CASE): what area(s) of the system this item is about, drawn from the controlled vocabulary in .context/instructions/theme-vocabulary.md. A multi-valued ORDERED list — themes[0] is the primary theme and the only member that participates in the read-protocol tie-break (below value then effort). Applies to type: PLAN, FINDING, and KNOWN_ISSUE (the action-candidate types); orthogonal to the magnitude axes (value/effort/severity). Required for those three types while status is DRAFT or ACTIVE (enforced by validate-context-frontmatter.sh, not this schema alone, since JSON Schema conditionals aren't evaluated by that validator). Written primary-first in block YAML style, like related.
